commit.message:
Updating `get_gpu_info()` to support AMD GPUs (#490)

This PR/commit updates the `get_gpu_info` routine (within `utils.py`) to support AMD GPUs.

The `get_gpu_info` routine now checks for the present of `rocm-smi` (equivalent of `nvidia-smi` for AMD GPUs) in the PATH
If found, then it will call `rocm-smi` to get the AMD GPU specific info,
Else if will call the `nvidia-smi` to get the NVIDIA GPU specific info.

Similar to the case for NVIDIA GPUs, the code assumes that if the system has multiple GPUs, then they are all the same.

Merge pull request #379 from leesharma:lee/fix-spacing

PiperOrigin-RevId: 290186419

Merge pull request #357 from reedwm:typo3

PiperOrigin-RevId: 290184656

Fix TF1 error.

After importing TF as "import tensorflow.compat.v1 as tf", you cannot accesss tf.compat.v1.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 290146847

Update README and fix a few TF2 compatibility issues.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 289177692

Change imports to be compatible with TF2.

Also call tf.disable_v1_behavior() at the bottom of runnable files for TF2 compatibility.

Also fix some lint errors. The linter complained that some files I modified had lint errors and forced me to fix them.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 288993605

Only import distort_image_ops from contrib if necessary.

Contrib is removed in TF2. It is now only imported if --distortions is enabled and --nodistort_color_in_yiq has not been enabled. in TF2, that case will instead cause an error to be thrown.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 288932758

Default --distortions to False.

This does not affect accuracy. The following command got 0.7592 accuracy without distortions and 0.7584 with distortions.

python tf_cnn_benchmarks.py --data_format=NCHW --batch_size=256 --model=resnet50 --optimizer=momentum --variable_update=replicated --distortions=$DISTORTIONS --gradient_repacking=8 --num_gpus=8 --num_epochs=90 --weight_decay=1e-4 --data_dir=/raid/imagenet-2012-tfrecord --use_fp16 --train_dir=/home/reedwm/train_dir

This improve performance. With the following command, performance increased from 9623 images/sec to 14362 images/sec on a GCP machine with n1-standard-96 GCE machine:

python tf_cnn_benchmarks.py --num_gpus=8 --batch_size=256 --data_dir=/data/imagenet/imagenet

Besides improving performance, the motivation is that distorting the colors in YIQ uses a contrib op, but contrib is not in TF 2. By defaulting --distortions to False, the default settings still will work in TF 2. Altneratively, I could have defaulted --distort_color_in_yiq to False, but this would have slightly hurt performance.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 288925175

Make all models that use contrib be TF1-only.

Models that use contrib will no longer be imported when TF2 is used. This is because TF2 no longer has contrib.

Currently, tf_cnn_benchmarks is incompatible with TF2 so this change doesn't has any effect yet. This change is a necessary step in supporting TF2.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 288749729

Remove some of the easier-to-remove uses of contrib.

This is to support TF2 in tf_cnn_benchmarks.

Subsequent changes will remove the remaining uses of contrib.

Use core batch norm instead of contrib.
